name: The Vegetarian Restaurant's Lemon Poppyseed Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In an electric mixer, blend the oil and 1 cup of Demerara sugar (or granulated sugar with a little molasses) until smooth. Add eggs one at a time, then milk and vanilla extract. Mix until well combined.
2. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt into a separate bowl. Gradually add this dry mixture to the wet ingredients on low speed, mixing just until blended.
3. Gently fold in the lemon zest using a spatula or knife. Pour batter into an oiled 6 to 8 cup bundt pan. Bake at 350°F for 40 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
4. To prepare the glaze, mix together the remaining Demerara sugar and lemon juice in a small saucepan over medium heat. Stir until the sugar is completely dissolved.
5. Remove the cake from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before turning it out onto a plate. Using a fork, create several holes throughout the surface of the cake. Slowly pour the glaze over the top, allowing it to drip down the sides. Let stand for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with slivers of lemon zest if desired. This recipe serves 8 to 10 people.
